rulings.Case Title: PETER ST. GEME et al VS. CHEVY CHASE BANK FSB, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Capital One, N.A.,Successor By Merger To Chevy Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions Of Peter St Geme And Polly St Geme And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE, N.A.'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Peter St Geme And Polly St Geme And For Sanctions (PART 2 OF 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING). Defendant shall notify Plaintiff of its selection no later than 12:00 noon on August 23, 2012. If either Plaintiff fails to provide such available dates by 12:00 noon on August 22, 2012, then Plaintiff Peter St. Geme's deposition will take place September 10, 2012 at 9:30 a.m. and Polly St. Geme's deposition will take place on September 11, 2012 at 9:30 a.m. Both depositions shall take place at the office of Defendant's counsel. Sanctions are awarded to Defendant and against Plaintiff Peter St. Geme only in the amount of $2,500. Sanctions are not awarded against Polly St. Geme. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to brad@kaplans.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: PETER ST. GEME et al VS. CHEVY CHASE BANK FSB, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Depositions Of Peter St Geme And Polly St Geme And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE, N.A.'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Peter St Geme And Polly St Geme And For Sanctions (PART 1 OF 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING). Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Each Plaintiff is to provide Defendant with three dates between August 27 and September 7, 2012 when they are available to have their deposition taken. The deposition will begin at 9:30 a.m. Such dates must be provided to Defendants no later than 12:00 noon on August 22, 2012. From these dates, Defendant shall choose the one for Peter St. Geme and the one for Polly St. Geme which is acceptable to it. TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED... =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: 209 POST STREET, L.P. VS. VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion Compelling Defendant Joanna Rees'S Responses To The First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Demands For Inspection, To Deem Facts Admitted, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2012, Line 3, Plaintiff'S Motion Compelling Defendant Joanna Rees'S Responses To The First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Demands For Inspection, To Deem Facts Admitted, And For Sanctions (PART 1 OF 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING). Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Defendant Joanna Reese shall respond to Plaintiff's Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set No. 1) and Request for Documents (Set No. 2) without objection by 12:00 noon on August 23, 2012. The facts identified in Plaintiff's Request for Admission (Set No. 2) to Joanna Rees are deemed admitted and the documents identified in the Request for Admission (Set No. 2) are deemed genuine. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant Joanna Reese in the amount of $2,000. TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED... =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ESPERANZA COTA ROMERO VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Plaintiffs have standing. The negligence claim is adequately pled. The second cause of action fails to state facts sufficient to state an intentional infliction of emotional distress claim. Given the nature of the allegations, since Defendants' actions were directed at the decedent and did not occur in Plaintiffs' presence, it is unlikely leave to amend can cure the defect. The third cause of action fails to allege facts with sufficient particularity to constitute a fraud claim. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege, with particularity, Defendants' intent to induce reliance on its misrepresentation and Plaintiffs' justifiable reliance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci Motion For Temporary Protective Order, Permanent Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI'S MOTION FOR TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER, PERMANENT PROTECTIVE ORDER (PART 2 OF 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING). Plaintiff should be prepared to address the specific prejudice it would suffer should it be required to maintain the confidentiality of documents the Defendant has agreed with a third party to not disseminate. With regard to section 8.1, both parties should be prepared to address whether their respective concerns can be alleviated by allowing Plaintiff to retain the confidential documents for some finite period of time after the conclusion of the litigation. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DARRELL BILBY VS. 30 MILLER PLACE ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANTs MARGARET SJOSTRAND, HERB YANOWITZ, JOHN PIERCE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Court grants the request to seal privileged matter in the request for judicial notice. The Court takes judicial notice of the State Worker's Compensation documents. However, Plaintiff's Worker's Compensation claim and the apparent existence of a policy of insurance do not conclusively establish that the policy provides coverage retroactive to the date of injury. The Complaint asserts Defendants lacked Workers' Compensation Insurance at the time of injury, and adequately alleges that Defendants employed Plaintiff. With respect to the remaining causes of action, Plaintiff's claims are not barred by the exclusive remedy provision set forth in Labor Code §132a. The Complaint, therefore, states facts sufficient to constitute the alleged claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JJM)
